full_name: PFC 5.0 Common Syntax Pitfalls (实战验证)
description: Real-world PFC5 syntax errors found and fixed during conjugate shear fracture experiment development. VERIFIED against PFC 5.0 help docs and live on pfc3d500_gui_64.exe.

sections.heading: Top-level variable assignment: use [var = value]
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 command-line DOES NOT accept plain 'var = value' or 'global var = value'.

PFC 5.0 (CORRECT):
  [sx0 = 0.050]
  [fj_emod = 20.0e9]
  [poro_target = 0.20]

PFC 5.0 (WRONG — will error with 'Bad conversion of parameter number 1'):
  sx0 = 0.050                    ; ERROR: not a command keyword
  global sx0 = 0.050              ; ERROR: global only valid inside define...end

Note: Inside a FISH define...end block, 'global var = value' IS correct and required.
  The [var = value] syntax evaluates a FISH expression inline at command level,
  which creates/assigns the global variable.

sections.heading: io.out() in command context: must wrap in [...]
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 does not accept io.out() as a standalone command-line statement.

PFC 5.0 (CORRECT):
  [io.out('message')]
  [io.out('Peak stress: ' + string(peak_axial_stress))]

PFC 5.0 (WRONG):
  io.out('message')              ; ERROR: Bad conversion of io.out

The [...] brackets evaluate the enclosed FISH expression at the command level.
Inside define...end blocks, io.out() is called directly without brackets.

sections.heading: FISH function calls at command level: @func vs [func]
ball group command syntax
sections.content:
PFC 5.0's ball group command differs from intuitive syntax.

PFC 5.0 (CORRECT):
  ball group 'layer1' range z -0.100 -0.050
  ball group 'test' slot 1 range z -0.100 0.000

The group name is a STRING argument following 'ball group'. Can be quoted or unquoted.

PFC 5.0 (WRONG):
  group layer1 range z -0.100 -0.050             ; ERROR: missing 'ball'
  ball group layer1 range z -0.100 -0.050        ; might work without quotes

Note: The help doc shows 'ball group <s> <<keyword ...>> <range>' where
  <s> is the group name, keywords are: add | remove | slot.

sections.heading: contact.type() returns STRING, not integer
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 FISH function contact.type(cp) returns a STRING like 'ball-ball', 'ball-facet'.

CORRECT:
  if contact.type(ct) # 'ball-ball' then continue   ; skip non-ball-ball

WRONG:
  if contact.type(ct) # 3 then continue             ; string never equals integer 3

Note: contact.type() returns: 'ball-ball', 'ball-facet', 'ball-pebble',
  'pebble-facet', 'pebble-pebble'.

sections.heading: Loop structures must be inside define...end
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 command-line does NOT support 'loop while', 'loop foreach', or 'do while'.

PFC 5.0 (CORRECT — wrap in a function):
  define run_loop
    local i = 1
    loop while i <= 5
      command
        restore bonded_homo
      end_command
      [conf_pressure = conf_list(i)]
      command
        call run_triaxial.p3dat
      end_command
      i = i + 1
    endloop
  end
  @run_loop

Note: Inside define...end, use 'command...end_command' blocks to issue PFC commands.
  The FISH 'command' block allows any PFC command-line syntax within.

sections.heading: ['name'] vs name in range group filters
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 range filters accept group names with or without quotes.

Both work:
  range contact type ball-ball group 'layer1'
  range contact type ball-ball group layer1

However, when the group name COULD be confused with a keyword, use quotes.
  range group wallassembly          ; unquoted, fine for simple names
  range group 'layer1'              ; quoted for clarity

sections.heading: set fishcall vs set fish callback
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 supports two forms for registering cyclic callbacks:

  set fishcall <time> @function     ; one-word form (used in fragmentation examples)
  set fish callback <time> @function ; two-word form (documented in pfc5-guide)

Both are equivalent. Examples:
  set fishcall 1.0 @servo_control
  set fish callback 1.0 @servo_control

sections.heading: FISH variable interpolation: @var and [expr]
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 command-line accepts both forms of variable interpolation:

  @var       — reference a FISH variable by name (simpler)
  [expr]     — evaluate any FISH expression (more powerful)

Examples:
  save result_[conf_pressure]
  set logfile triaxial_[conf_pressure].log
  wall attribute zvelocity [ax_vel] range id 1        ; positive velocity
  wall generate id 2 plane dip 0 dd 0 position 0 0 [sz0+0.003]

The [expr] form is needed for expressions (e.g., [sz0+0.003]).
The @var form works for simple variable references (@fj_emod).

Note: Inside 'command...end_command' blocks within FISH functions, always use [expr].

IMPORTANT: See the separate 'Negative values with [expr]: use [-(expr)]' entry
for the correct way to write negative values.

sections.heading: Verified: Flat-Joint property pb_radius
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 Flat-Joint model stores pb_radius as a contact property, accessible via:
  contact.prop(ct, 'pb_radius')

This works for both linearpbond and flatjoint contact models. The fracture size
used in the fragmentation fracture.p3fis uses contact.prop(ct,'pb_radius').

VERIFIED: This property is available on flatjoint contacts.

sections.heading: history 语法：id N @func 和 add 两种形式都有效
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 支持两种创建 history 的语法（已验证于 rocktest 示例）：

简写形式（推荐，rocktest 使用）：
  history id 1 @axial_stress
  history id 2 @axial_strain
  history id 3 @crack_total

完整形式（与文档语法表一致）：
  history add id 1 fish @axial_stress
  history add id 2 fish @axial_strain

内置关键字使用 mechanical age（不要用 mech age 缩写）：
  history id 1 mechanical age          ; 正确
  history id 1 mech age                ; 可能出错，不推荐

导出 history 到文件：
  history write 1 2 3 file 'results.his'     ; 空格分隔的 ID，不需要 id 关键字

sections.heading: Python API: ball.count(group_name) is valid
sections.content:
In PFC 5.0 Python API, itasca.ball.count() with a group name argument returns
ball count in that group:

  itasca.ball.count()                  ; total ball count
  itasca.ball.count('layer1')          ; balls in group 'layer1'

There is NO itasca.ball.BallFilter in PFC 5.0's Python API.
  itasca.ball.BallFilter.by_group()    ; AttributeError

sections.heading: Negative values with [expr]: use [-(expr)], not -[expr]
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 command-line does NOT accept -[expression] for negative values.
The '-' minus sign outside the brackets is parsed as a separate token,
causing 'Bad conversion of parameter number N' errors.

PFC 5.0 (CORRECT — put the minus INSIDE the brackets):
  wall generate id 2 plane dip 0 dd 0 position 0 0 [-(sz0+0.003)]
  box [-(sx0)] [sx0] [-(sy0)] [sy0] [-(sz0)] [sz0]
  wall attribute zvelocity [-(ax_vel)] range id 1

PFC 5.0 (WRONG — minus outside brackets):
  wall generate id 2 plane dip 0 dd 0 position 0 0 -[sz0+0.003]      ; ERROR
  box -[sx0] [sx0] -[sy0] [sy0] -[sz0] [sz0]                        ; ERROR
  wall attribute zvelocity -[ax_vel] range id 1                       ; ERROR

Reason: [expression] evaluates to a single token (the numeric value).
The '-' before the bracket is NOT absorbed into the bracket token.
The parser sees two separate tokens: '-' and '0.103'.

VERIFIED on pfc3d500_gui_64.exe with error:
  'Bad conversion of parameter number 11 (0). Expected tokens: 3D vector of floating point values'

sections.heading: Project structuring: save/restore/call chain pattern
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 多文件项目的标准串联模式：每个 .p3dat 文件有且只有一对 restore/save，通过 call 串联。

核心原则：
  1. 每个 .p3dat 文件要么开头 new（构建流程），要么开头 restore（后 processing 流程）
  2. 每个 .p3dat 文件结尾必须有 save
  3. 主控文件只 call 子文件，自己不内嵌 restore/save 对
  4. .p3fis 是 FISH 函数库，不含 restore/save

文件串联示例：

  A.p3dat（构建）：
    new
    ; ... 构建模型 ...
    save model_state_a

  B.p3dat（后处理）：
    restore model_state_a    ← 和 A 的 save 配对
    ; ... 修改/处理 ...
    save model_state_b

  main.p3dat（主控串联，不内嵌 restore/save）：
    call A.p3dat
    call B.p3dat
    call C.p3dat

参数文件模式：
  在 save 之前先加载 FISH 函数库（.p3fis），使 sav 文件自带函数：

  install.p3dat：
    restore unbonded_specimen
    ; ... 安装接触模型 ...
    call triaxial_servo.p3fis    ← 在 save 前加载 FISH
    call fracture.p3fis
    call engine.p3fis
    [conf_pressure = 0.0]         ← 设置默认值
    save bonded_specimen          ← sav 内嵌 FISH 函数

  test.p3dat（独立可运行）：
    restore bonded_specimen       ← FISH 函数随 sav 恢复
    [conf_pressure = 10.0e6]      ← 覆盖默认值
    @run_test                     ← 直接调用（无需 call .p3fis）
    save failed_10MPa

优势：
  - 每个文件可单独运行（在 GUI 中 open → call）
  - 主控文件通过 call 线性串联，无额外状态管理
  - sav 文件自包含（模型状态 + FISH 函数 + 参数默认值）
  - 避免同一个文件内出现多个 restore/save 对

sections.heading: PFC5 FISH: table.keys() does NOT exist; use arrays instead
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 FISH does NOT support table.keys() or similar key iteration.
Table is a numeric x/y data series (interpolation curve), not a key-value map.

PFC5 FISH table functions (confirmed in pfc5help):
  table.create(name)       ; create named table, returns pointer
  table.size(tab)          ; number of data points
  table.name(tab)          ; table name
  table(tab, x)            ; read y-value at x (with interpolation)
  table(tab, x) = y        ; write data point (creates or overwrites)

Raw index access (table_x / table_y) was NOT verified against pfc5help.
For iterating paired data, use FISH arrays instead:

  local n = array.size(arr_keys)
  local i = 1
  loop while i <= n
      local key = arr_keys(i)
      local val = arr_vals(i)
      i = i + 1
  endloop

Note: array and table are different data structures.
  array.create(n) / a(i)  — array, numeric index (1-based)
  table.create(name)      — table, xy interpolation curve, no key iteration

sections.heading: FISH inline if-then-else: avoid = inside 'then' clause; use block if instead
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 FISH does NOT accept inline 'if...then...else...endif' when the 'then' clause
contains an assignment with '='. It triggers 'fish :extraneous equals sign(s)' error.

PFC 5.0 (CORRECT — block if):
  define ew_xx
    if wlx0 > 0.0
      ew_xx = (wlx - wlx0) / wlx0
    else
      ew_xx = 0.0
    endif
  end

PFC 5.0 (WRONG — inline then with =):
  define ew_xx
    if wlx0 > 0.0 then ew_xx = (wlx - wlx0) / wlx0 else ew_xx = 0.0 endif    ; ERROR
  end

Also wrong — inline then with local variable assignment:
  if bin > n_bins then bin = n_bins endif    ; ERROR: extraneous equals sign

The error does NOT occur when 'then' clause has NO assignment:
  if mode = 1 then exit    ; OK
  if x > 0 then continue   ; OK

Safe rule: always use block if (if/endif on separate lines) when the body
contains any '=' assignment. Inline 'then' is only safe for jump statements
(continue, exit) or simple function calls without '='.
